behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real model output quality (speaker diarization/label preservation) from speech-to-text. A stubbed transcript would hide diarization failures (MOCK-HIDE, LLM-DEP/AI-CALL-DEFAULT). Rejected Workflow because behavior text targets one model stage: "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels"; it does not require validating multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run transcription on real meeting audio with multiple speakers and assert transcript speaker labels remain correctly attached to utterances used for attribution downstream.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is parser logic over transcript text to structured fields; failure can be reproduced at one function/object boundary with no real I/O (MOCK-MISS indicates local logic). Rejected Integration because behavior text is specifically "parsing extracts ... into ... structure," not cross-module state propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative transcript snippets to the parsing function and assert extracted tuples contain correct assignee and task text in the summary-input schema.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on LLM summary decisions (coverage and attribution of action items). Deterministic fixtures can test wiring but cannot validate model judgment (LLM-DEP, deterministic-assertion trap). Rejected Workflow because this assertion is about one live summary result quality criterion, not validating multiple distinct agentic handoff decisions end-to-end.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcript content with known assignees/tasks, call the real summarization model, and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets deterministic routing logic after decisions are identified; this is local transformation/schema-mapping logic. Rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on "routes ... into the `decisions` section schema" and does not require real infra or model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Given structured inputs marked as decisions, test routing/mapping function places entries in `decisions` and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is model-output content quality: whether key decisions are surfaced and categorized correctly in generated summary. Stubs would mask failures in model extraction/classification behavior (LLM-DEP, AI-CALL-DEFAULT). Rejected Workflow because behavior text asks for one output property of the live summary, not multi-step agentic pipeline correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run real summarization on transcripts containing explicit decisions; assert those decisions are present in `decisions` section and absent as only narrative mentions.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Requires multiple agentic steps in sequence (per-segment transcription/summarization plus merge coherence). Failures can emerge at inter-step handoff and accumulation across segments (MULTI-STEP + LLM-involved orchestration). Rejected Agentic because behavior explicitly includes chained operations: "splits ... segments ... sequentially, merging results."
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording, execute true segmentation pipeline, process each segment through real transcription/summarization calls, then assert merged summary is coherent and preserves cross-segment action items/decisions without duplication or loss.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit end-to-end multi-stage pipeline behavior over real recording with real model calls and delivery orchestration. Real failures arise from sequential stage interactions and agentic outputs across stages (REAL-INFRA + MULTI-STEP). Rejected System because behavior text includes multiple AI-dependent stages, so system-only infra testing is insufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end production-like job on a real meeting file from upload to delivered structured summary, asserting unattended completion and valid final schema with populated action items/decisions/attributions.
